message:
feat(ingest): tick 196 persist_captured_segment_with_cx fully cx-threaded (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Continues the ft-xbnl0.2.3 cx-threading sweep. The
`persist_captured_segment_with_cx` wrapper previously only did a
pre-flight cx.checkpoint() and then delegated to the ambient-cx
`persist_captured_segment`, which meant every inner storage call
(record_gap, append_segment, truncation gap, discontinuity gap)
still picked up cx via `Cx::current()` thread-local lookup — so a
mid-ingest cancel from the caller's explicit cx wasn't observed
until the whole chain drained.

Tick 196 inlines the body and routes every storage call through
its `_with_cx` sibling:
  - `storage.record_gap_with_cx(cx, ...)` for the initial gap
  - `storage.append_segment_with_cx(cx, ...)` for the main write
  - `storage.record_gap_with_cx(cx, ...)` for the truncation gap
  - `storage.record_gap_with_cx(cx, ...)` for the discontinuity gap

Combined with the tick-176 send_with_cx propagation into each mpsc
backpressure reserve, an operator who cancels mid-ingest now bails
at the next await boundary instead of blocking until the capture
chain drains.

Legacy `persist_captured_segment` is preserved unchanged for
ambient-cx callers and non-asupersync builds.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/ingest.rs:
  - persist_captured_segment_with_cx inlines the full body with
    cx-threaded storage calls
  - docstring updated to describe the tick-196 upgrade and the
    new cancellation semantics

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
